channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: ICE acting director Todd Lyons will resign at end of May, DHS says
item.description: U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement acting director Todd Lyons, a key executor of President Donald Trump's mass deportations agenda, will resign at the end of May, federal officials announced.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement acting director Todd Lyons, a key executor of President Donald Trump's mass deportations agenda, will resign at the end of May, federal officials announced.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Singer D4vd is arrested months after a teen's remains were found in his car
item.description: The 21-year-old Houston-born singer, whose real name is David Burke, had been under a secret investigation by an LA County grand jury after a 14-year-old girl's decomposed body was found in his car.
item.content:encoded:
The 21-year-old Houston-born singer, whose real name is David Burke, had been under a secret investigation by an LA County grand jury after a 14-year-old girl's decomposed body was found in his car.

(Image credit: Jordan Strauss/Invision/AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: House passes bill extending protections for Haitian migrants in the U.S.
item.description: Ten Republicans voted alongside Democrats, in a rebuke to the Trump administration's immigration policies. Should it pass the Senate, the White House said President Trump would veto the measure.
item.content:encoded:
Ten Republicans voted alongside Democrats, in a rebuke to the Trump administration's immigration policies. Should it pass the Senate, the White House said President Trump would veto the measure.

(Image credit: Lynne Sladky)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Georgia jail uses drones to block dangerous deliveries to inmates
item.description: The Fulton County jail in Georgia is using drones to intercept deliveries of dangerous contraband to inmates. The drones are made by Flock, a company under scrutiny for its surveillance reach.
item.content:encoded: The Fulton County jail in Georgia is using drones to intercept deliveries of dangerous contraband to inmates. The drones are made by Flock, a company under scrutiny for its surveillance reach.
item.dc:creator: Chamian Cruz

item.title: These musicians are providing the soundtrack for anti-ICE protests in LA
item.description: Los Jornaleros Del Norte play protest songs whose lyrics reflect the hopes and struggles of undocumented workers as they evade immigration agents patrolling the streets.
item.content:encoded:
Los Jornaleros Del Norte play protest songs whose lyrics reflect the hopes and struggles of undocumented workers as they evade immigration agents patrolling the streets.

(Image credit: Adrian Florido)
item.dc:creator: Adrian Florido

item.title: Minnesota has charged an ICE officer with assault for alleged actions during immigration surge
item.description: Hennepin County officials say these are the first charges filed against a federal immigration agent related to the crackdown that brought thousands of federal officers to the state. The widespread operation led to the shooting deaths of two American citizens.
item.content:encoded:
Hennepin County officials say these are the first charges filed against a federal immigration agent related to the crackdown that brought thousands of federal officers to the state. The widespread operation led to the shooting deaths of two American citizens.

(Image credit: Mark Vancleave)
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: A jury declared Live Nation a monopoly. But ticket prices won't drop just yet
item.description: D.C. and 33 states now have to argue in favor of specific remedies and fines, which could be paused if Live Nation appeals. Experts say the long-term impact on ticket prices isn't clear either.
item.content:encoded:
D.C. and 33 states now have to argue in favor of specific remedies and fines, which could be paused if Live Nation appeals. Experts say the long-term impact on ticket prices isn't clear either.

(Image credit: Paul Sakuma)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Israel starts a tense ceasefire in Lebanon, as Trump sounds optimistic on Iran talks
item.description: The leaders of Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Israel's fight against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon, but Israel vowed to keep troops in southern Lebanon.
item.content:encoded:
The leaders of Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire in Israel's fight against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon, but Israel vowed to keep troops in southern Lebanon.

(Image credit: Hassan Ammar)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: 'Dear America': HUD workers say they're being blocked from doing their jobs
item.description: A website with anonymous employee letters accuses the Trump administration of undermining work on housing discrimination. HUD says it's restoring "sanity" to fair housing enforcement.
item.content:encoded:
A website with anonymous employee letters accuses the Trump administration of undermining work on housing discrimination. HUD says it's restoring "sanity" to fair housing enforcement.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: 3 things to know about naval blockades as the U.S. patrols the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The White House says it wants to choke off Iran's oil export revenue. But experts say that blockades are often unpredictable and difficult to enforce.
item.content:encoded:
The White House says it wants to choke off Iran's oil export revenue. But experts say that blockades are often unpredictable and difficult to enforce.

(Image credit: Jesse Monford)
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman
